source: Promoted from 3 real Weekly Menu Planner .docx files (residence: Earlwood, June 2026 cycle), analysed 2026-07-04. See FRAME-LEDGER.md row 4. Updated 2026-07-04 with Stuart's answers on organisation, process state, rotation, template, and roster. Updated 2026-07-05 with roster volatility and per-house dinner-sharing conditionality.
privacy_note: Documentation is internal (Stuart, 2026-07-04) - privacy anonymisation lifted for this repo. Real resident names now used. Still avoid publishing this repo externally without re-checking that footing.

organisation.name: Danny Met Sally (DMS)
organisation.note: Confirmed 2026-07-04. Explains the docx core-property creator field 'dannymetsally' seen on 2 of 3 source files - same org, not a coincidence. Nationwide, estimated 20-30 SIL homes; sticking with the residences already known (Earlwood populated, 4 others as placeholders) rather than chasing the full list for now.

current_state.process: fully manual today - Debbie hand-builds one Weekly Menu Planner .docx per resident, per 28-day period
current_state.unit_of_work: one meal plan = one resident x one 28-day (4-week) period
current_state.automation_goal: Harvest pipeline generates the same artifact per resident, so Debbie can plan across the entire fleet of residences/residents instead of one home at a time
current_state.scale_driver: support-worker rostering (see support_worker_roster below) is what currently limits Debbie to doing this by hand per home; automating it is the actual unlock for fleet-wide planning

cycle.note: 28-day period, confirmed the standard unit for one resident's plan

dinner_rotation.sharing_condition: in a house where residents' dietary requirements are compatible, all residents can eat the same dinner (Earlwood's observed pattern). Where dietary requirements clash (allergens, texture modification, medical diets), a shared dinner is impracticable and residents need individual dinners instead. So 'one shared dinner per house' is NOT a company-wide rule - it's an outcome of whichever residents happen to live together, evaluated per house.
dinner_rotation.shared_across_residences: not applicable as originally framed - the real question is per-house dietary compatibility, not a nationwide policy. Deferred, not blocking.
dinner_rotation.source_evidence: identical dinner text across Earlwood's 3 residents for corresponding weeks/days, e.g. all three had the same dish on Monday of week 1 - consistent with their dietary requirements being compatible enough to share
dinner_rotation.cadence_rule: confirmed by Stuart 2026-07-04: today the rotation's cadence is arbitrary/manual ('whenever') - it is NOT a fixed real-world constraint the frame must preserve. Automation is free to set the rotation cycle however it wants; do not treat the observed 4-week/28-recipe pattern as a hard requirement.
dinner_rotation.generator_implication: the generator must decide per-house, per-period whether residents can share a dinner rotation or need individual ones, driven by each resident's dietary/constraint-block profile - not assume either way.

support_worker_roster.type: NEW axis, added 2026-07-04 - support workers are rostered on/off shift by day, by week, by month
support_worker_roster.implication: the 'Created with Support Worker' field and the community-participation marker's named-worker variant are both roster lookups, not free text - a generator needs the actual shift roster as an input, not just a static name
support_worker_roster.volatility: clarified 2026-07-05: the roster is highly changeable - named staff can change at short notice. A meal plan that bakes in a specific support worker's name can go stale quickly. Implication: this is not a fact to freeze into a static document at generation time; the generator (especially any 'live' tier) needs to read the roster close to when the plan is used, not once and cache it indefinitely.
support_worker_roster.why_it_matters: this is the real scale bottleneck: automating roster-aware plan generation is what lets Debbie cover the whole fleet instead of one resident/home at a time

dislikes.shape: comma-separated food items, paragraph before the week heading

may_include: medical directives (weight targets, medication timing, exercise mandates)

sil_day_flag.type: per-day marker, currently embedded in the day-column header text rather than a structured field
observed_patterns: rotates week to week (different days flagged each of the 4 weeks) - Kathryn Briggs
fixed set of days every week (always Tue/Thu/Sat/Sun) - Vanessa Ryan
absent entirely (no SIL flag on any day) - Teresa

community_participation_marker.type: inline prefix within a meal cell, not a separate column
community_participation_marker.note: three different spellings/conventions across 3 residents. Given the standard-template ruling below, treat this as per-resident/drift, not three real company conventions - normalise to one vocabulary in the generator, backed by the support-worker roster.

conditional_substitution.type: prose rule embedded in a cell

running_quota.type: prose rule embedded in a cell, tracking a daily/weekly cap
running_quota.example_pattern: (only if under N for the day)

eat_out_placeholder.type: cell value delegating the meal to an outing or support worker instead of specifying food

template_ruling.implication: the two observed footer/document-control table shapes (see footer_template_variation below) are NOT two real template lineages - they are per-resident variation or drift on top of one canonical template. The generator should target ONE footer shape and treat the 1x2 variant as something to reconcile, not preserve.

residences.name: Earlwood
residences.status: populated - source data available, 3 real residents

constraint_block.sil_day_pattern: absent - no SIL flag on any day

residences.name: Berala
residences.status: unbuilt - no source data, placeholder residents only; deferred per Stuart 2026-07-04 (sticking with known residences for now)

open_questions: Where does the support-worker shift roster live, and in what format (spreadsheet? rostering system? manual)? Made more urgent by its volatility (2026-07-05) - a short-notice-changeable roster means the generator needs a live/current lookup, not a one-time import.

How does the generator determine whether a house's residents have dietary requirements compatible enough to share one dinner rotation, vs needing individual dinners? Is this something Debbie assesses manually today, and could that assessment be captured as data per resident?
How many residents typically live in one SIL home (Earlwood = 3; unconfirmed as typical)
Full list of residences/resident counts nationwide - deferred, sticking with Earlwood + 4 placeholders for now
